ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Drone Rotom,' art (small yellow ghost-like creature with four drone rotor blades), and item-card function all match the canonical Rotom Drone form as established in Pokemon Sword & Shield (Game Freak, 2019), where Rotom inhabit drones to film Gym Challenge battles. The base species Rotom is National Pokedex no. 479 (Electric/Ghost, Generation IV). The Drone form is a non-battling utility role distinct from the five standard appliance forms (Heat/Wash/Frost/Fan/Mow Rotom). Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh4-151) and Bulbapedia: Rotom Drone via WebSearch snippet.

trivia_bullets.text: Drone Rotom (no. 151/185) is an Item Trainer card from Vivid Voltage (SWSH4), released November 13, 2020 in English (first appeared in the Japanese Amazing Volt Tackle expansion). The underlying species, Rotom, is National Pokedex no. 479, an Electric/Ghost-type introduced in Generation IV (Pokemon Diamond and Pearl, Game Freak, 2006). [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-151; WebSearch: Bulbapedia Drone Rotom (Vivid Voltage 151); WebSearch: Rotom species Pokedex 479]
sources: WebSearch: Bulbapedia Drone Rotom (Vivid Voltage 151) via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Rotom's signature mechanic across the mainline games is possessing household appliances to take five alternate battling forms: Heat Rotom (oven, Electric/Fire), Wash Rotom (washing machine, Electric/Water), Frost Rotom (refrigerator, Electric/Ice), Fan Rotom (fan, Electric/Flying), and Mow Rotom (lawnmower, Electric/Grass). The Drone form used in Sword & Shield is a sixth, non-battling role: Rotom inhabit drones to film live Gym Challenge and League matches at Wyndon Stadium. Five Drone Rotom also expelled colored smoke to form the World Coronation Series logo above Wyndon in the Pokemon Journeys anime. [WebSearch: pokemondb.net Rotom forms; WebSearch: Bulbapedia Rotom Drone via WebSearch snippet]
sources: WebSearch: Bulbapedia Rotom Drone via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Effect as an Item card: force the opponent to reveal their hand; if they do, look at the top card of their deck. Multiple Item cards may be played in the same turn. This is a low-commitment information-gathering card — the opponent chooses whether to expose their hand, but if they decline, the top-deck-peek is forfeited. No flavor text printed on this card. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-151 rules]

Rotom's TCG history includes several non-Pokemon Trainer-card appearances (Rotom Phone, Rotom Dex, Drone Rotom) that use the species as a technology-interface framing device rather than a battling Pokemon. This pattern — the Ghost-type species that possesses machines becoming itself instrumentalized as an in-game utility tool — is a consistent design choice across the Sun & Moon and Sword & Shield TCG eras, and a source of community discussion about Rotom's ambiguous status as simultaneously a Pokemon species and an appliance/tool. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia Rotom Pokedex; WebSearch: Rotom forms list rotomlabs.net]
sources: WebSearch: Bulbapedia Rotom (Pokemon) via WebSearch snippet
